steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that 'Mère Poussepin' marks a significant change in Gwen John's artistic development. Supporting evidence must show a before-and-after shift in her own style — not external opinion or influence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “*Mère Poussepin* has been analyzed extensively since it was first exhibited, as no two viewers seem to agree about exactly what the painting means.”: Describes how critics read the painting, not a change in John's technique or approach. ✗
  • Choice B — “Of all John's paintings, none so clearly represents her abilities and ideas as *Mère Poussepin*.”: Speaks to the painting's place in her career as a whole, but does not establish a stylistic before-and-after. ✗
  • Choice C — “The paintings that John produced before *Mère Poussepin* can be thought of as belonging to her earlier style, to which she never returned.”: Directly states that her prior paintings belonged to an earlier style she permanently abandoned, proving the claimed shift in artistic development. ✓
  • Choice D — “Although John was clearly influenced by other artists of her time, she was also an artist ahead of her time, as *Mère Poussepin* demonstrates.”: Credits outside forces rather than demonstrating an actual change in her own work. ✗
C is correct.
